title: Prior-Product Audit, Maintenance Reality, and Reframed Challenge
description: A successful first project has dependency drift, unresolved feedback, stale assumptions, hidden operations work, and a proposed sequel based only on more features. Learners cumulatively produce maintenance audit and reframed product charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.
Classify feedback by user outcome, evidence source, frequency, severity, reach, confidence, and strategic fit.
Measure dependency, defect, support, content, data, infrastructure, and cognitive maintenance cost before adding scope.
